meta.name: Ghanaian Light Soup
meta.subtitle: Clear, spicy tomato-pepper soup with aromatics and optional goat, beef, or fish

source.name: ssa
notes: Canonical Ghana-style 'light soup' (nkwan) base: tomatoes, onions, peppers, ginger/garlic.
Designed for SSA planners: adaptable protein, heat-scalable, and stew-and-starch serving model.

yield.notes: Serves 6 with fufu/banku/rice balls; serves 8 as a starter.

ingredients.label: Protein (optional): goat, beef, or chicken pieces
notes: Goat is classic; beef and chicken are common.
If using fish, see the variation and add later to avoid breaking apart.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes (Roma/plum), chopped
ingredients.notes: Or substitute 1 can (28 oz) crushed tomatoes.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Optional but recommended for deeper color and flavor.

ingredients.label: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Optional but common; adds sweetness and body.

ingredients.label: Hot pepper (scotch bonnet or habanero)
notes: Use whole for controllable heat; blend for stronger heat.
Start with 1 pepper if you’re sensitive to heat.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
ingredients.notes: Optional; some versions start with a small amount of oil for the base.

ingredients.label: Stock (chicken/beef) or water
ingredients.notes: Light soup is meant to be thinner/clearer than groundnut or palm nut soups.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust based on stock and protein seasoning.

ingredients.label: Shrimp powder or fermented locust beans (dawadawa/iru) (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional traditional umami booster; use lightly.

ingredients.label: Lime or lemon (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional squeeze at the end for brightness.

steps.title: Blend the tomato-pepper base
instructions: In a blender, combine onion, tomatoes, garlic, ginger, bell pepper (if using), and hot peppers.
Blend until smooth. Add a splash of water only if needed to move the blender.
For milder heat: keep one hot pepper whole and do not blend it.
checks: Base is smooth with no large chunks.

steps.title: Start the soup and build the broth
instructions: Heat the pot over medium heat. If using oil, add it and warm briefly.
Add tomato paste (optional) and stir 1–2 minutes to toast it lightly.
Pour in the blended base carefully.
Add thyme, bay leaves (optional), black pepper, and shrimp powder/dawadawa (optional).
Simmer uncovered 10–15 minutes to cook out rawness and develop flavor.
checks: Base smells cooked (not raw) and darkens slightly.

steps.title: Add stock and simmer until protein is tender
instructions: Add stock/water and bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
If using goat/beef/chicken: add it now (or add pre-cooked pieces).
Simmer until the protein is tender and the broth tastes cohesive.
Skim foam if needed for a cleaner broth.
checks: Protein is tender (goat/beef may need longer depending on cut).

steps.title: Adjust seasoning and final consistency
instructions: Taste and add salt gradually (stock varies).
If the soup is thicker than desired, add a bit more water/stock to keep it 'light'.
Optional: add a squeeze of lime/lemon for brightness right before serving.
checks: Consistency is brothy and pourable (not thick).

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot with fufu, banku, kenkey, or rice balls (omotuo).
For extra heat, serve with a small side of fresh pepper sauce.

fridge.notes: Cool quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours. Reheat gently to preserve broth clarity.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight; reheat gently and adjust salt after reheating.

reheat.notes: Reheat at a gentle simmer; add a splash of water/stock if needed.

remixNotes: Thin leftovers and serve over rice for a fast meal.
Add fresh herbs (cilantro/parsley) at serving if desired.

notes: Light soup should remain brothy—avoid reducing too aggressively after adding stock.
Heat control tip: use whole hot peppers and remove once the soup reaches your preferred heat.
If using fish, add during the last 8–10 minutes to prevent it from breaking apart.

adapt.notes: Use firm fish steaks/fillets. Simmer base and stock first; add fish for the last 8–10 minutes. Keep simmer gentle.
Goat often needs longer simmer (45–90 minutes depending on cut). Add additional water/stock as needed to keep it brothy.
Use 1 hot pepper whole (do not blend). Remove after 10 minutes of simmering with stock.
